sanskrit: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे । देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
hindiTranslation: दान देना कर्तव्य है  ऐसे भावसे जो दान देश, काल और पात्रके प्राप्त होनेपर अनुपकारीको दिया जाता है, वह दान सात्त्विक कहा गया है ॥
englishTranslation: A gift given with the feeling that giving is a duty, offered to a worthy person at the right place and time, is called sattvic.
essence: Giving becomes pure when nothing is being bought in return.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A gift is pure when it is given as a duty, to the right person, at the right time and place, without expecting anything back.
simpleInsight: Generosity feels lighter when it is free of self-interest.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna still shaken and Krishna teaching the nature of faith, the discussion turns to giving. After rejecting prideful and harmful charity, Krishna defines the clearest form of giving: timely, fitting, and free from expectation.
modernRelevance: You send money to a friend who will never repay you, or help quietly without posting it anywhere. The act feels clean because it was never a transaction.
translationLiteral: दातव्यम् (to be given) / इति (thus) / यत् (which) / दानम् (gift) / दीयते (is given) / अनुपकारिणे (to one who does not return a favor) / देशे (in place) / काले (in time) / च (and) / पात्रे (to a worthy recipient) / च (and) / तत् (that) / दानम् (gift) / सात्त्विकम् (sattvic) / स्मृतम् (is remembered as)
